business.title: Engineers solved an airflow mystery hidden nearly a mile underground
business.desc: Engineers at a deep underground research facility noticed something strange during major rainstorms: airflow underground sometimes reversed direction. Using new sensors and mathematical modeling, they found that water rushing down a shaft was effectively pushing air through the tunnels like a giant piston. The breakthrough explains a long-standing mystery and could help underground operations better predict and manage ventilation during storms and emergencies.

technology.title: Preparing for Andes virus outbreaks to prevent transmission in health care settings
technology.desc: Andes virus, the type of hantavirus responsible for the outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship earlier this year, has a fatality rate of up to 36%. Precautions such as isolation of infected individuals and use of personal protective equipment by health care personnel can prevent transmission if implemented at the earliest stages of infection. But because human-to-human transmission of Andes virus was detected for the first time only in 1996, and because outbreaks occur infrequently, clinicians worldwide were unfamiliar with Andes virus clinical presentation, epidemiological risk factors and infection prevention strategies when the cruise ship outbreak occurred.
